summary: PVF dispatchers still resolve sender-chosen handler strings with Call Compile; init already precompiles SRVFNC*/CLTFNC* globals, so a validated missionNamespace lookup can close the dispatch trust boundary without changing the high-level protocol.
nextAction: Patch source Chernarus dispatchers to use allowlisted missionNamespace getVariable lookup, then smoke valid server/client commands plus forged invalid command rejection.

summary: The client Tactical menu gates ICBM locally, but a forged RequestSpecial payload can reach the server ICBM branch and spawn NukeDammage without server-side commander/upgrade/funds validation.
nextAction: Implement the ICBM playbook server-side: re-derive side/team/commander, validate upgrade/module/funds/object state, debit server-side and reject malformed payloads.

summary: Side-supply mutation is a direct temp publicVariable path for west/east; DR-22 shows overspend can become a windfall and DR-44 shows the same channel is directly client-writable without a server-verifiable requester.
nextAction: Clamp negative west/east supply floors and validate temp-channel side/amount first; broader DR-44 authority needs server-owned state or a request shape with a verifiable requester anchor.

summary: ATTACK_WAVE_INIT bypasses PVF and trusts client-supplied supply/side, allowing forged discounts that can drive side-wide unit prices to zero or negative.
nextAction: Treat ATTACK_WAVE_INIT as a request, re-derive server side supply, validate requester/side, clamp modifier/duration and preserve documented JIP behavior.

summary: Construction, defense, upgrades, player buys, structure sale, gear/EASA/service, supply rewards and WASP HQ recovery repeat the same client- or payload-authoritative spend/effect pattern.
nextAction: Do not patch these as isolated UI bugs. Pick a server-ledger/request model, then migrate flows in roadmap order with dedicated smoke records.

summary: Current source Chernarus still uses random varQueu token churn and the crewless branch still exits before the normal local queue decrement; public queu broadcast reduction remains a separate UI-aware follow-up.
nextAction: Patch source Chernarus, propagate generated Vanilla, then run Arma smoke for repeated crewless/crewed buys, concurrent buyers and factory-dead cleanup. Leave public queu broadcast reduction separate.

Patch the command-center scan in source Chernarus, propagate Vanilla, smoke command-center delivery/no-completion near unrelated objects, then continue cooldown casing, reward/source authority and vehicle handler idempotency.
summary: Supply mission authority cleanup remains open, and the command-center scan sub-step is not patched in current source: the 80-meter scan still uses a broad class list before Base_WarfareBUAVterminal filtering; the 8-meter nearby-player scan stays broad by design.

Current source Chernarus has the paratrooper marker sender and client handler file, but HandleParatrooperMarkerCreation is still absent from the client PVF registration list; Arma smoke and modded drift cleanup remain.
nextAction: Add the client PVF registration in source Chernarus, propagate Vanilla, smoke paratrooper drop marker creation in Arma 2 OA and decide divergent modded folder handling separately.

summary: MASH tents may still work for respawn, but MASH marker sync is not proven live: server relay code exists, the client receiver compile is commented, and no live client broadcast of the creation channel was found.
nextAction: Either revive with a server-held marker list, unique names and server-held resend/pull for JIP clients, or remove the dead relay/receiver path intentionally.

summary: Current source Chernarus still calls Skill_Init.sqf at :547 and :571, then calls WFBE_SK_FNC_Apply at :572; Arma smoke remains after a source patch.
nextAction: Remove the duplicate second Skill_Init call in source Chernarus, propagate Vanilla, then smoke Soldier/non-Soldier AI caps, respawn skill reapply and dedicated/JIP client init.

summary: Current source Chernarus still checks isDedicated inside both FPS publisher scripts instead of exiting non-dedicated hosts before the loops; publisher consolidation remains a separate decision.
nextAction: Patch early non-dedicated exits in source Chernarus, propagate Vanilla, then smoke dedicated RHUD/server FPS publishing plus hosted/listen no-spin behavior. Defer publisher consolidation.

summary: WASP marker naming still has a sleepless display-54 wait in current source Chernarus; the display-12 sibling already uses sleep 0.1. Map-marker smoke remains after a source patch.
nextAction: Patch display-54 wait throttling in source Chernarus, propagate Vanilla, then smoke map double-click/Enter/Escape marker naming behavior and timeout/no-dialog input re-enable.

summary: Dialog/title resource IDD reuse is confirmed, including EASA/Economy sharing 23000 and title resources sharing 10200; normal dialog flow mitigates some risk, but future findDisplay/debug/control lookups can hit the wrong display.
nextAction: Assign unique display/title IDs, audit findDisplay assumptions, then smoke EASA, Economy, RscOverlay and OptionsAvailable flows.

summary: Current source Chernarus still assigns _side = _this in Server_AssignNewCommander.sqf, and RequestNewCommander.sqf still sends the duplicate caller-side new-commander-assigned notification.
nextAction: Patch source Chernarus, propagate Vanilla, then run hosted/dedicated/JIP commander reassignment smoke. Keep requester/role validation separate.

summary: AI commander constants/state and upgrade worker exist, but no proven live scheduler starts full autonomous commander production/logistics or sets the running flag end-to-end.
nextAction: Owner should decide revive/remove/defer before autonomous AI supply-heli/logistics work; player-run PR #1 supply heli should remain separate.

summary: Public-variable and PVF networking channels now have source-backed sender/receiver/payload mapping with JIP and authority boundaries, plus unsafe-channel candidates added to Feature-Status-Register.
evidence: Source scan found authoritative transport dispatch and direct publicVariable writes across missionNamespace and side-effect channels

No handler ownership or validation existed for a few dangerous channels (notably wfbe_supply_temp_*, ATTACK_WAVE_INIT sender/receiver assumptions, and MASH/paratrooper direct handlers), all captured with file references in docs
Direct channel table now separates confirmed-safe, risky, and likely-racy flows for future refactoring.

summary: Miksuu PR #10/#11/#12 prove supply mission start logic has regressed through exploit/UX/JIP iterations; future variants need server-owned validation and late-join tests.
nextAction: Keep supply start/reward hardening server-owned; add JIP and remote-start smoke to implementation PRs.

summary: Miksuu town AI performance work needed follow-up activation restoration and defender filtering; future optimizations should not reduce scans without preserving wake signals and diagnostics.
nextAction: Require active-town, pre-capture, remote-player attack and defender-bleed smoke checks for town-AI scan changes.
